inflation-cpi.desc: The annual inflation rate in Hungary rose to 2.1% in April 2026 from 1.8% in March, marking a three-month high. The acceleration was driven mainly by higher food prices, which rose to 1.5% after stalling in March, and a rebound in other goods, including other items and fuels (0.1% vs -0.7%). trade-balance.desc: Hungary’s trade surplus narrowed to EUR 924 million in March 2026 from EUR 1,697 million in the corresponding month a year ago, while surpassing market forecasts of EUR 665 million, as exports rose less than imports. unemployment.desc: The unemployment rate in Hungary rose to 4.7% in January-March 2026 from 4.3% the corresponding period a year ago. The number of unemployed individuals increased by 13.6 thousand year-on-year to 226.4 thousand.
